text:
On a sunny afternoon, Oliver discovered an old telescope perched atop the hill behind his house. Assembling it wasn't easy; rust flaked off each joint, and stars seemed to laugh at him. After countless trips and falls, the telescope finally pointed skyward.

Oliver peered through the eyepiece. He saw crinkles in the cloud-cover - maybe they were galaxies? He chuckled to himself; he'd never lived for telescopes, but this was oddly exciting.

His cat, Whiskers, padded over. "Hey, Mister Owl," Whiskers said. "You not seeing something?"

He shook his head. "I don't think so, I only see constellations."

Whiskers barked affectionately. Oliver thought maybe they were kindred spirits. Later, a flash caught his eye. It was a shooting star... maybe three? Maybe ten.

He laughed. This odd hobby was weird, but kinda awesome.

For days afterward, Oliver watched
